questions.question: How do you initiate the creation of a new database template?

options.text: Click the 'Settings' gear icon and select 'Create Template'
options.explanation: Templates are created directly within the database view, not from the general settings gear.

options.text: Click the dropdown arrow next to 'New' and select '+ New template'
options.explanation: The article explicitly states to click the dropdown arrow next to 'New' at the top right and select '+ New template'.

options.text: Right-click on an existing page and choose 'Turn into Template'
options.explanation: There is no option to convert a specific page directly into a template using right-click.

options.text: Drag a property card from the sidebar into the database body
options.explanation: Dragging property cards is used to rearrange properties, not to create templates.

questions.question: What happens if you fill in a Relation property when creating a database template?

options.text: It creates a dynamic link to the page currently being edited
options.explanation: Templates usually create links based on the content of the new page, but filling the relation manually overrides this behavior.

options.text: Every page created from that template will relate to the same existing page(s)
options.explanation: The article notes that if you add a relation property to a template, do not fill it in unless you want every page created with that template to relate to the same page(s).

options.text: It automatically assigns the creator of the template to the relation
options.explanation: Relation properties do not auto-assign based on the creator by default; manual entry is required.

options.text: It breaks the template and prevents it from being used
options.explanation: Filling in a relation property does not break the template; it just forces a static relationship for all instances.

questions.question: Which of the following frequencies is NOT supported for repeating database templates?

options.text: Daily
options.explanation: Daily is explicitly listed as a supported frequency for repeating templates.

options.text: Weekly
options.explanation: Weekly is explicitly listed as a supported frequency for repeating templates.

options.text: Hourly
options.explanation: The article states you can repeat daily, weekly, monthly, or yearly. Hourly is not an option.

options.text: Yearly
options.explanation: Yearly is explicitly listed as a supported frequency for repeating templates.

questions.question: What is the maximum allowed nesting level for database templates?

options.text: 2 levels
options.explanation: While nesting is complex, the article explicitly states a limit of three levels.

options.text: 3 levels
options.explanation: The article states that you can only have three levels of nesting per database template.

options.text: 4 levels
options.explanation: The article does not support a 4th level; the limit is three.

options.text: No limit
options.explanation: There is a specific hard limit of three levels mentioned in the 'Nesting database templates' section.

questions.question: How can you edit an existing database template?

options.text: Double-click the template name in the dropdown menu
options.explanation: There is no double-click edit option for template names in the dropdown menu.

options.text: Open the dropdown next to 'New' and click 'Edit' via the '•••' menu
options.explanation: The article instructs to open the '•••' menu next to the template and click Edit.

options.text: Navigate to the database settings page and select 'Edit Template'
options.explanation: Templates are edited directly from the database view via the dropdown menu, not from a separate settings page.

options.text: You cannot edit existing templates after creation
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that you can edit, duplicate, or delete existing templates.

questions.question: Are database templates available across all databases in a workspace?

options.text: Yes, templates are shared automatically across all databases
options.explanation: Templates are local to the specific database where they were created. Each database can define its own templates.

options.text: Yes, but only if you copy and paste the template content
options.explanation: Notion does not share templates automatically; you must create them in each new database individually.

options.text: No, they are only available in the specific database where you created them
options.explanation: The FAQ explicitly answers this: 'No, they're only available in the specific database where you created them.'

options.text: Only if the workspace owner has enabled workspace-wide templates
options.explanation: The article describes creating templates within a specific database, not a global workspace setting.

questions.question: What determines the name of a database template?

options.text: The property names defined in the template
options.explanation: Property names define the structure, not the template's display name.

options.text: The title of the page used to create the template
options.explanation: The article states: 'Whatever you title this page will be the name of the template you use going forward.'

options.text: The database it is located in
options.explanation: While the bar indicates the database location, the template name comes from the page title.

options.text: The user who created the template
options.explanation: The creator's name is not used for the template's display name.

questions.question: When creating a repeating database template, where do you select the 'Repeat' option?

options.text: From the main database settings menu
options.explanation: You must access the specific template's options to enable repeating behavior.

options.text: Next to the 'Repeat' button in the database header
options.explanation: The 'Repeat' dropdown is accessed within the context of the specific template, not the general database header.

options.text: Next to the specific database template via the '•••' menu
options.explanation: The steps specify: 'Next to the database template you want to repeat, select •••. Open the dropdown next to Repeat.'

options.text: At the bottom of the 'New' dropdown menu
options.explanation: The 'Repeat' option is found inside the template's contextual menu, not the main 'New' dropdown.

questions.question: What is a key limitation regarding nesting templates within recurring templates?

options.text: You cannot nest templates within templates that recur daily
options.explanation: The article notes: 'You can't nest a template within a template that recurs daily. You can only nest a template within a template that recurs weekly, monthly, or yearly.'

options.text: You cannot nest a template within any recurring template
options.explanation: You *can* nest within weekly, monthly, or yearly recurring templates; daily is the exception.

options.text: You cannot nest more than one template inside another
options.explanation: While nesting is complex, the specific limitation mentioned is about the frequency (daily) rather than the count of nesting.

options.text: You cannot nest templates if the recurring interval is longer than a year
options.explanation: The limitation is strictly about the 'daily' recurrence type, not the duration.

questions.question: Which of the following can be included in a database template?

options.text: Only text and properties
options.explanation: Templates can contain more diverse content types.

options.text: Images, embeds, and sub-pages
options.explanation: The article states: 'Templates can contain any type of content, including images, embeds, and sub-pages.'

options.text: Only properties and relations
options.explanation: While properties and relations are supported, so are images, embeds, and sub-pages.

options.text: Only formulas and rollups
options.explanation: Formulas and rollups are properties, but the article mentions visual content like images as well.

questions.question: Which menu button allows you to Duplicate or Delete an existing template?

options.text: The 'Settings' gear icon
options.explanation: The settings gear icon is not used for duplicating or deleting templates.

options.text: The '•••' menu next to the specific template
options.explanation: The article states: 'The same ••• menu also lets you duplicate or delete a template.'

options.text: The 'New' dropdown arrow
options.explanation: The 'New' dropdown is used to select/create templates, not to manage existing ones.

options.text: The 'Repeat' dropdown
options.explanation: The 'Repeat' dropdown is for scheduling, not deleting.

questions.question: When setting up a repeating template, what interval units can you specify?

options.text: Days and Weeks
options.explanation: While daily and weekly are frequencies, the intervals for non-daily repeats are weeks, months, or years.

options.text: Weeks, Months, or Years
options.explanation: The article states: 'Indicate how many weeks, months, or years you want in between repeats.'

options.text: Hours and Days
options.explanation: The supported intervals are weeks, months, or years, not hours.

options.text: Minutes, Hours, or Days
options.explanation: Time-based intervals (minutes/hours) are not supported for repeating templates.
